canonical_endpoint.per_order_formula: IF(risk_active, IF(APj > 0, APj / LTj, 0.5 * (1 / RPj)), 1 - ((Bt + Ut) / j))
canonical_endpoint.aggregation: Unclipped mean over completed non-lost workbook-visible rows; lost and horizon-unresolved orders remain in the cumulative Bt/Ut ledger and lost orders are a simultaneous non-inferiority guardrail.
canonical_endpoint.decision: Binding primary endpoint for the current Paper 2 success definition; service loss, quantity ReT, lost orders, worst-node service, tail risk and resource ledgers remain mandatory guardrails.

garrido_factory_cobb_douglas_2024.scope: Factory-level aggregate production planning in an MTS facility over 36 weeks; demand variability is the sole uncertainty; seven fixed pure APP substrategies are compared.

variables.tau: average time/effort to meet net requirements
variables.kappa_dot: strategy cost normalized across the seven strategies

garrido_factory_cobb_douglas_2024.calibration: Each term was set to contribute 0.2 at its maximum observed across 10,000 simulations; the coefficients are calibrated to the paper's hypothetical factory and strategy ensemble, not to the MFSC distribution system.
garrido_factory_cobb_douglas_2024.dominant_static_warning: The paper reports S12 (match-chase with spare capacity) as the dominant fixed substrategy, median index 0.55784. It does not establish sequential state feedback or learning value.

repository_cd_implementations.reason: Uses published exponents but maps them to a stylized two-CSSU distribution adapter, fixes phi and kappa at 1, and is explicitly not paper-calibrated for MFSC.
repository_cd_implementations.current_evidence: Program G confirms a metric-induced policy-ranking reversal: the CD winner loses canonical order ReT, attended orders and worst-CSSU service.

repository_cd_implementations.reason: The default calibration file stores DES-rebalanced exponents but activates variance_log balancing, under which the score uses standardized log variables rather than the published coefficients; it also uses a post-hoc de-saturation constant. Its APP cost mapping omits hiring, firing and overtime terms absent from the DES.

The published coefficients and sigmoid are exact as a mathematical transformation; applying them to MFSC variables still requires an independently validated variable ledger and the paper's seven-strategy cost normalization or an explicitly new calibration.

limitations: not a complete full-horizon open-loop frontier
current PPO did not beat the best static policy

war_phi4_psi1_5.service_direction: PPO worse: flow fill 0.4283 vs 0.8090; lost rate 0.5288 vs 0.0593; Excel ReT 0.0007 vs 0.0024.

metric_decision.current_paper2: Cobb-Douglas may be reported as a frozen secondary construct-sensitivity outcome. It cannot rescue a null or loss on ret_excel_visible_v1 and cannot satisfy the current Paper 2 success definition by itself.
metric_decision.separate_factory_resilience_lane: Scientifically possible only as a separately preregistered factory/APP study with validated APP ledgers, published or independently justified calibration, complete full-horizon APP comparators, and canonical ReT/lost-order/worst-node/resource non-inferiority retained as cross-construct guardrails when embedded in MFSC.
metric_decision.other_metrics: No alternative metric may be selected after observing policy rankings. Any new primary construct requires domain authority, a frozen estimand and calibration, and a separate claim; it cannot be called thesis ReT.

risk_governance.allowed: Thesis risks may be edited within author-supported ranges, and a realistic new risk may be introduced as a disclosed researcher extension.
required_before_screen: source or exact Garrido sign-off question
plausible envelope frozen independently of policy results
risk_governance.forbidden: Increasing frequency or impact until a learner or preferred metric wins, or reusing the prior phi4/psi1.5 war cell as thesis-faithful evidence.
